message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- algorithmic-art: Creating algorithmic art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter exploration. Use this when users request creating art using code, generative art, algorithmic art, flow fields, or particle systems. Create original algorithmic art rather than copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
- brand-guidelines: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.
- canvas-design: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. TRIGGER when: code imports anthropic/@anthropic-ai/sdk; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (/v1/agents, /v1/sessions, /v1/environments). DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks.
- doc-coauthoring: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.
- docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- internal-comms: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase validate/gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- mcp-builder: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).
- pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- slack-gif-creator: Knowledge and utilities for creating animated GIFs optimized for Slack. Provides constraints, validation tools, and animation concepts. Use when users request animated GIFs for Slack like "make me a GIF of X doing Y for Slack."
- theme-factory: Toolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.
- web-artifacts-builder: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- statusline: Set up Claude Code's status line UI
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
- insights: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
- team-onboarding: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

content.content: Launching skill: libnn-optimizer

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/nick/.claude/skills/libnn-optimizer

# libnn Optimizer

Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
Six-phase FSM: validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
State persisted in `opt/state.json`.

## Commands

- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run Phase 0: config validation (pass -> gather, fail -> report)
- `/libnn-optimizer gather <pattern>` - Run Phase 1: resolve function via pattern match, parse task spec + target features
- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration
- `/libnn-optimizer status` - Show current state from opt/state.json
- `/libnn-optimizer reset` - Reset state to `validate` phase

Arguments received: ``

## Project Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `$CWD/opt/config.toml` | Target config (user-owned, NEVER modify) |
| `$CWD/opt/state.json` | Optimization state (LLM reads/writes) |
| `$CWD/opt/script/` | Validation and helper scripts |
| `$CWD/opt/fpga_config/` | FPGA Verilog .inc files |
| `$CWD/opt/patches/` | Per-iteration diffs (audit trail) |
| `$CWD/opt/build/` | Build artifacts |
| `$CWD/opt/log/` | Build/test/perf logs |
| `$CWD/plan.md` | Full implementation plan |
| `/home/nick/work/libnn/` | libnn source tree |

## Phase 0: Validate

Initial FSM phase. Validates config.toml and all referenced resources before any optimization work.

**Entry condition:** `phase == "validate"` (initial state) or `/libnn-optimizer validate` command.

```bash
python3 $CWD/opt/script/validate_config.py --strace
```

**On pass ("All steps passed"):**
1. Print full output to the user.
2. Write/update `opt/state.json` setting `phase` to `"gather"`.
3. Report: validation passed, ready for gather phase.

**On fail (any step fails):**
1. Print full output to the user.
2. Keep `phase` as `"validate"` in state.json (or do not advance).
3. Report which steps failed. Do NOT proceed to gather.

## Phase 1: Gather

Parse task spec and target features. No builds.

0. **Resolve function name** from user-supplied pattern:
   ```bash
   python3 $CWD/opt/script/resolve_function.py <pattern>
   ```
   - Exit 0 (one match): use the printed function name, proceed.
   - Exit 2 (multiple matches): show the numbered list to user, ask them to pick. Re-run with a narrower pattern or use the exact name.
   - Exit 1 (no match): report error, do NOT proceed.

   Pattern examples: `conv_dw u8 asym`, `relu_s8`, `sigmoid`.

1. **Read config.toml.** Extract toolchain PATH, QEMU binary, FPGA host:port, CPU, VLEN.

2. **Read FPGA .inc file** (path from `[target].fpga_config`, relative to `opt/`). Extract:
   - Vector: `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
   - D-cache: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`
   - I-cache: `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
   - L2: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
   - ISA: `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
   - Bus: `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`

   Parse using regex: `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+(\d+) `` for integers, `` `define\s+<KEY>_INT\s+(\d+) `` or `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+"(yes|no)" `` for booleans.

3. **Read source_input** (perf test C file):
   Parse `#define` block from `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c`. Extract shapes (INPUT_X, INPUT_Y, INPUT_CH, KERNEL_X, etc.), quantization params (OUT_SHIFT, OUT_MULT, offsets, activation min/max), buffer sizes. Resolve function name from `riscv_nn_*()` call site.

4. **Locate baseline source** at `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path.

5. **Derive optimization macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>`.

6. **Write state.json.** Set `phase` to `"baseline"`.

**Verification:** state.json must contain:
- All shapes from the perf test `#define` block
- `target_features.vlen == 1024`
- `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`
- `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`
- Correct `source_file` path under `Source/`

## Phase 2: Baseline

Measure unmodified library on target.

1. Rebuild baseline:
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
   ```

2. QEMU correctness (strace mode for semihosting fd 9):
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
   ```
   Accept only "accuracy checking pass". Failure aborts -- baseline is broken.

3. FPGA perf measurement:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf && cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
   ```
   Parse `performance.csv` for cycles and instructions.

4. Write `baseline` to state.json. Initialize empty `history`. Set `phase` to `"iterate"`.

## Phase 3: Iterate

One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.

Per iteration:

1. **Plan** - Inspect current best source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change. Record in `notes`.

2. **Edit** - Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`: add optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`. Replace prior iteration's block if present. Baseline `#else` stays byte-for-byte intact.

3. **Build** - Rebuild `libnn.a` twice:
   - Without macro (sanity -- baseline still compiles)
   - With `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` for perf

4. **Correctness** - QEMU strace test. Fail -> `accepted=false`, skip perf.

5. **Perf** - `test_perf.sh`. Collect cycles/instructions.

6. **Commit** - Git commit in libnn tree. Snapshot diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch`.

7. **Accept/reject** - `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.

8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.

## Phase 4: Evaluate

1. Restore `best.commit` in libnn worktree.
2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.
3. Rerun FPGA perf, confirm within +/- 0.5% of `best.cycles`.
4. Emit `opt/report.md`: baseline -> best cycles, speedup, commit SHA, rationale.
6. Set `phase` to `"done"`.

## Phase 5: Done

Terminal. Human reviews `opt/report.md`. Winning commits in libnn tree are the deliverable.

## State Machine

Read `opt/state.json` field `phase` to determine where to resume:

```
validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate (loop) -> evaluate -> done
```

## State File Format

```json
{
  "mode": "single_function",
  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c",
  "source_file": "Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c",
  "macro": "ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>",
  "phase": "validate|gather|baseline|iterate|evaluate|done",
  "iteration": 0,
  "baseline": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0},
  "best": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "iteration": 0, "commit": ""},
  "history": [],
  "shapes": {},
  "quant_params": {},
  "data_types": {"input": "", "weight": "", "output": ""},
  "buffer_sizes": {},
  "target_features": {}
}
```

## Key Rules

1. **Never modify `opt/config.toml`** -- user-owned configuration.
2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
3. **QEMU is for correctness only** -- FPGA cycle counts are ground truth.
4. **Primary target: AX45MPV VLEN=1024** -- do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 perf.
5. **Use `NDS_VEC_*` macros only** in `ENA_VEC_ISA` blocks. Never `__riscv_*` intrinsics.
6. Macro naming: `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
7. Validation is Phase 0 of the FSM. Gather phase requires `phase == "gather"` (validation must have passed).
8. Scripts are the single source of truth for builds and tests -- never construct gcc/QEMU/GDB commands manually.
Let me check the current state.

# libnn Optimizer

Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
Six-phase FSM: validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
State persisted in `opt/state.json`.

## Commands

- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run Phase 0: config validation (pass -> gather, fail -> report)
- `/libnn-optimizer gather <pattern>` - Run Phase 1: resolve function via pattern match, parse task spec + target features
- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration
- `/libnn-optimizer status` - Show current state from opt/state.json
- `/libnn-optimizer reset` - Reset state to `validate` phase

Arguments received: `baseline`

## Project Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `$CWD/opt/config.toml` | Target config (user-owned, NEVER modify) |
| `$CWD/opt/state.json` | Optimization state (LLM reads/writes) |
| `$CWD/opt/script/` | Validation and helper scripts |
| `$CWD/opt/fpga_config/` | FPGA Verilog .inc files |
| `$CWD/opt/patches/` | Per-iteration diffs (audit trail) |
| `$CWD/opt/build/` | Build artifacts |
| `$CWD/opt/log/` | Build/test/perf logs |
| `$CWD/plan.md` | Full implementation plan |
| `/home/nick/work/libnn/` | libnn source tree |

## Phase 0: Validate

Initial FSM phase. Validates config.toml and all referenced resources before any optimization work.

**Entry condition:** `phase == "validate"` (initial state) or `/libnn-optimizer validate` command.

```bash
python3 $CWD/opt/script/validate_config.py --strace
```

**On pass ("All steps passed"):**
1. Print full output to the user.
2. Write/update `opt/state.json` setting `phase` to `"gather"`.
3. Report: validation passed, ready for gather phase.

**On fail (any step fails):**
1. Print full output to the user.
2. Keep `phase` as `"validate"` in state.json (or do not advance).
3. Report which steps failed. Do NOT proceed to gather.

## Phase 1: Gather

Parse task spec and target features. No builds.

0. **Resolve function name** from user-supplied pattern:
   ```bash
   python3 $CWD/opt/script/resolve_function.py <pattern>
   ```
   - Exit 0 (one match): use the printed function name, proceed.
   - Exit 2 (multiple matches): show the numbered list to user, ask them to pick. Re-run with a narrower pattern or use the exact name.
   - Exit 1 (no match): report error, do NOT proceed.

   Pattern examples: `conv_dw u8 asym`, `relu_s8`, `sigmoid`.

1. **Read config.toml.** Extract toolchain PATH, QEMU binary, FPGA host:port, CPU, VLEN.

2. **Read FPGA .inc file** (path from `[target].fpga_config`, relative to `opt/`). Extract:
   - Vector: `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
   - D-cache: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`
   - I-cache: `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
   - L2: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
   - ISA: `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
   - Bus: `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`

   Parse using regex: `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+(\d+) `` for integers, `` `define\s+<KEY>_INT\s+(\d+) `` or `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+"(yes|no)" `` for booleans.

3. **Read source_input** (perf test C file):
   Parse `#define` block from `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c`. Extract shapes (INPUT_X, INPUT_Y, INPUT_CH, KERNEL_X, etc.), quantization params (OUT_SHIFT, OUT_MULT, offsets, activation min/max), buffer sizes. Resolve function name from `riscv_nn_*()` call site.

4. **Locate baseline source** at `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path.

5. **Derive optimization macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>`.

6. **Write state.json.** Set `phase` to `"baseline"`.

**Verification:** state.json must contain:
- All shapes from the perf test `#define` block
- `target_features.vlen == 1024`
- `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`
- `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`
- Correct `source_file` path under `Source/`

## Phase 2: Baseline

Measure unmodified library on target.

1. Rebuild baseline:
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
   ```

2. QEMU correctness (strace mode for semihosting fd 9):
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
   ```
   Accept only "accuracy checking pass". Failure aborts -- baseline is broken.

3. FPGA perf measurement:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf && cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
   ```
   Parse `performance.csv` for cycles and instructions.

4. Write `baseline` to state.json. Initialize empty `history`. Set `phase` to `"iterate"`.

## Phase 3: Iterate

One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.

Per iteration:

1. **Plan** - Inspect current best source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change. Record in `notes`.

2. **Edit** - Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`: add optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`. Replace prior iteration's block if present. Baseline `#else` stays byte-for-byte intact.

3. **Build** - Rebuild `libnn.a` twice:
   - Without macro (sanity -- baseline still compiles)
   - With `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` for perf

4. **Correctness** - QEMU strace test. Fail -> `accepted=false`, skip perf.

5. **Perf** - `test_perf.sh`. Collect cycles/instructions.

6. **Commit** - Git commit in libnn tree. Snapshot diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch`.

7. **Accept/reject** - `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.

8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.

## Phase 4: Evaluate

1. Restore `best.commit` in libnn worktree.
2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.
3. Rerun FPGA perf, confirm within +/- 0.5% of `best.cycles`.
4. Emit `opt/report.md`: baseline -> best cycles, speedup, commit SHA, rationale.
6. Set `phase` to `"done"`.

## Phase 5: Done

Terminal. Human reviews `opt/report.md`. Winning commits in libnn tree are the deliverable.

## State Machine

Read `opt/state.json` field `phase` to determine where to resume:

```
validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate (loop) -> evaluate -> done
```

## State File Format

```json
{
  "mode": "single_function",
  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c",
  "source_file": "Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c",
  "macro": "ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>",
  "phase": "validate|gather|baseline|iterate|evaluate|done",
  "iteration": 0,
  "baseline": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0},
  "best": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "iteration": 0, "commit": ""},
  "history": [],
  "shapes": {},
  "quant_params": {},
  "data_types": {"input": "", "weight": "", "output": ""},
  "buffer_sizes": {},
  "target_features": {}
}
```

## Key Rules

1. **Never modify `opt/config.toml`** -- user-owned configuration.
2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
3. **QEMU is for correctness only** -- FPGA cycle counts are ground truth.
4. **Primary target: AX45MPV VLEN=1024** -- do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 perf.
5. **Use `NDS_VEC_*` macros only** in `ENA_VEC_ISA` blocks. Never `__riscv_*` intrinsics.
6. Macro naming: `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
7. Validation is Phase 0 of the FSM. Gather phase requires `phase == "gather"` (validation must have passed).
8. Scripts are the single source of truth for builds and tests -- never construct gcc/QEMU/GDB commands manually.

Running Phase 2: Baseline.

**Step 1: Build unmodified libnn**

input.description: Check QEMU test script for toolchain path

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Build and run a single libnn unit test on Andes QEMU (AX45MPV)
#
# Usage:
#   run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> [--strace] [--vlen N] [--timeout N]
#
# Examples:
#   run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
#   run_qemu_test.sh relu_s8 --strace --vlen 512
#
# Notes:
#   - In Claude Code's sandbox, QEMU semihosting output goes to fd 9, not
#     stdout. The --strace flag uses strace to capture fd 9 writes and parse
#     "accuracy checking pass/fail".
#   - QEMU must be run from Examples/unit_func/ because test binaries open
#     golden data via relative paths (e.g., "bin/8b_in1.bin").
################################################################################
set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

# Defaults
USE_STRACE=0
VLEN=1024
TIMEOUT=120
TEST_NAME=""

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        -*)        echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
        *)         TEST_NAME="$1"; shift ;;
    esac
done

# Build and run a single libnn unit test on Andes QEMU (AX45MPV)
#

#

# Paths
TOOLCHAIN_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
QEMU_BIN="/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
QEMU_CFG="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"

# Validate
for f in "${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/riscv64-elf-gcc" "${QEMU_BIN}" "${QEMU_CFG}" "${TEST_SRC}"; do
    if [ ! -f "$f" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Not found: $f" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}:${PATH}"
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

# Check libnn.a exists
LIBNN="${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
if [ ! -f "${LIBNN}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: libnn.a not found. Run build_libnn.sh first." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Compile
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"
CFLAGS="-O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections"
CFLAGS+=" -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS"
CFLAGS+=" -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0"
CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"
INC="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include"
ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.adx"

echo "[compile] ${TEST_NAME}"
${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${INC} -o "${ADX}" "${TEST_SRC}" "${LIBNN}" -lm 2>&1
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "RESULT: COMPILE_ERROR" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Run QEMU
# Must run from unit_func dir so semihosting relative file opens work
cd "${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"

QEMU_CMD="${QEMU_BIN} -readconfig ${QEMU_CFG} -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=${VLEN} -nographic -semihosting -bios ${ADX}"

echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=${VLEN} (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)"

if [ "${USE_STRACE}" -eq 1 ]; then
    # Use strace to capture semihosting writes on fd 9
    STRACE_LOG="${WORK_DIR}/strace_${TEST_NAME}.log"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Build and run a single libnn unit test on Andes QEMU (AX45MPV)
#
# Usage:
#   run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> [--strace] [--vlen N] [--timeout N]
#
# Examples:
#   run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
#   run_qemu_test.sh relu_s8 --strace --vlen 512
#
# Notes:
#   - In Claude Code's sandbox, QEMU semihosting output goes to fd 9, not
#     stdout. The --strace flag uses strace to capture fd 9 writes and parse
#     "accuracy checking pass/fail".
#   - QEMU must be run from Examples/unit_func/ because test binaries open
#     golden data via relative paths (e.g., "bin/8b_in1.bin").
################################################################################
set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

# Defaults
USE_STRACE=0
VLEN=1024
TIMEOUT=120
TEST_NAME=""

# Parse arguments
while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
        -*)        echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
        *)         TEST_NAME="$1"; shift ;;
    esac
done

if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--strace] [--vlen N] [--timeout N]" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Paths
TOOLCHAIN_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
QEMU_BIN="/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
QEMU_CFG="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg"
UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"

# Validate
for f in "${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/riscv64-elf-gcc" "${QEMU_BIN}" "${QEMU_CFG}" "${TEST_SRC}"; do
    if [ ! -f "$f" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: Not found: $f" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
done

export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}:${PATH}"
mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"

# Check libnn.a exists
LIBNN="${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
if [ ! -f "${LIBNN}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: libnn.a not found. Run build_libnn.sh first." >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Compile
CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"
CFLAGS="-O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections"
CFLAGS+=" -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS"
CFLAGS+=" -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0"
CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"
INC="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include"
ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.adx"

echo "[compile] ${TEST_NAME}"
${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${INC} -o "${ADX}" "${TEST_SRC}" "${LIBNN}" -lm 2>&1
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "RESULT: COMPILE_ERROR" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Run QEMU
# Must run from unit_func dir so semihosting relative file opens work
cd "${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"

QEMU_CMD="${QEMU_BIN} -readconfig ${QEMU_CFG} -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=${VLEN} -nographic -semihosting -bios ${ADX}"

echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=${VLEN} (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)"

if [ "${USE_STRACE}" -eq 1 ]; then
    # Use strace to capture semihosting writes on fd 9
    STRACE_LOG="${WORK_DIR}/strace_${TEST_NAME}.log"
    timeout "${TIMEOUT}" strace -f -e trace=write -o "${STRACE_LOG}" ${QEMU_CMD} > /dev/null 2>&1
    QEMU_RC=$?

    # Parse strace output for semihosting messages (fd 9 or fd 1)
    # The fd used by QEMU semihosting can vary across environments
    OUTPUT=$(grep -E 'write\((9|1),' "${STRACE_LOG}" 2>/dev/null | sed 's/.*write([0-9]*, "//; s/\\n".*//; s/".*//') || true

    echo ""
    echo "--- semihosting output ---"
    echo "${OUTPUT}"
    echo "--- end ---"
    echo ""

    # Check for pass/fail
    if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qi "accuracy checking pass"; then
        PASS_COUNT=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -ci "accuracy checking pass" || true)
        FAIL_COUNT=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -ci "accuracy checking fail\|accuracy checking FAIL" || true)

        if [ "${FAIL_COUNT}" -gt 0 ]; then
            echo "RESULT: FAIL (${FAIL_COUNT} failures detected)"
            exit 1
        else
            echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-tests passed)"
            exit 0
        fi
    elif [ "${QEMU_RC}" -eq 124 ]; then
        echo "RESULT: TIMEOUT (${TIMEOUT}s)"
        exit 1
    else
        echo "RESULT: UNKNOWN (no pass/fail detected, QEMU exit=${QEMU_RC})"
        # Dump last 20 lines of strace for debugging
        echo "--- strace tail ---"
        tail -20 "${STRACE_LOG}" 2>/dev/null || true
        echo "---"
        exit 1
    fi
else
    # Direct run (output may not appear due to fd 9 issue)
    timeout "${TIMEOUT}" ${QEMU_CMD} 2>&1
    QEMU_RC=$?
    if [ "${QEMU_RC}" -eq 124 ]; then
        echo "RESULT: TIMEOUT (${TIMEOUT}s)"
        exit 1
    fi
    exit ${QEMU_RC}
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Build libnn for AX45MPV with RVV (VLEN=1024)
# Output: libnn.a and libnn.objdump at the repo root
################################################################################
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"

# Andes toolchain
TOOLCHAIN_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
if [ ! -d "${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Toolchain not found at ${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}" >&2
    exit 1
fi
export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}:${PATH}"

echo "Building libnn for AX45MPV (VLEN=1024)..."
cd "${LIB_ROOT}"
/usr/bin/bash build.sh AX45MPV
ret=$?

if [ $ret -eq 0 ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Build successful."
    echo "  Library: ${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
    echo "  Objdump: ${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.objdump"
else
    echo "Build FAILED (exit code $ret)" >&2
fi

exit $ret

APP_CFLAGS="-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -Wl,-gc-sections -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
    INC_DIR="-I$(${COMPILER_NAME} -print-sysroot)/../../riscv64-elf/include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
    ADX_FILE="t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.adx"

    build_cmd="${COMPILER_NAME} ${APP_CFLAGS} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_FILE} ${SRC_FILE} ${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -lm"
    log "Build: ${build_cmd}"
    ${build_cmd} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} -ne 0 ]]; then
        echo "Error: compilation failed for ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
        exit 1

file.content:
BUILD_LIB_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}"
if [[ ! -f "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn.a" ]]; then
    echo "Error: libnn.a not found at ${BUILD_LIB_DIR}. Run build.sh first."
    exit 1
fi
# The linker flag -lnn_v expects libnn_v.a. build.sh produces libnn.a, so
# create a symlink with the expected name.
ln -sf "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn.a" "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn_v.a"
# Prepend -L so the linker finds our library before the toolchain's built-in.
CPU_FLAGS="-L${BUILD_LIB_DIR} ${CPU_FLAGS}"

##### main ######
rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
log "***************************************************************************************"
log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
    log "SINGLE_FUNC: ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
fi
log "***************************************************************************************"

EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"

if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
    #####################################################################

content.text:
Good question. Looking at the Phase 0 validate script output:

```
[OK] Symlinked riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython -> riscv64-elf-gdb
```

The validate script creates the symlink in a temp dir and adds it to PATH for its own run. But that temp dir gets cleaned up after validation finishes, so when `test_perf.sh` runs later, the symlink is gone.

The FPGA board runner (`gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`) spawns `riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython` specifically - it's an expect script that needs a GDB without Python to avoid output interference. Since this toolchain only ships `riscv64-elf-gdb` (which happens to have no Python anyway), we just need the symlink to exist.

I created it at `/home/nick/work/libnn/.local_bin/riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython` and prepended that to PATH. The FPGA perf run is executing now in background.

FPGA run failed. Two issues:
1. Board port mismatch - `test_perf.sh` uses port 1115, but config.toml says 1119
2. `Cannot access memory at address 0xf0100008` - L2C setup failure

Let me check the port configuration in test_perf.sh.

message.content: Let's update the 'baseline' phase in skill.  Most steps could be done by python script to reduce the uncertainty of llm

content.text:
Good call. The baseline phase had 4 failure modes that were all deterministic - a script handles them reliably. Let me plan this out.

**Problems the LLM hit that a script avoids:**
1. Wrong toolchain PATH (config.toml knows the right one)
2. Missing `-I Include/` in test_perf.sh single-func mode
3. Wrong FPGA port (config.toml knows 1119, script defaults to 1115)
4. Missing `gdb-nopython` symlink

Let me first review the current skill definition and the existing scripts to understand the full scope.

input.prompt:
I need to understand the existing infrastructure for the libnn-optimizer skill's baseline phase. Explore these files and report their key details:

1. `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/validate_config.py` - How does it read config.toml? What toolchain/QEMU/FPGA setup does it do? What's the gdb-nopython symlink logic?

2. `/home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh` - Full flow: how it builds, runs QEMU, parses results. Note the toolchain path it uses.

3. `/home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/build_libnn.sh` - How it builds libnn.

4. `/home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh` - Focus on: how MYPC/MYPORT are set, how single-func mode works (lines ~263-300), what INC_DIR it uses, how it parses performance results from board output, and how it writes performance.csv.

5. `/home/nick/work/agentic_libnn_opt/opt/script/resolve_function.py` - Just skim to see the pattern of how scripts read config.toml.

6. `/home/nick/personal_git/.claude/claude_skills/skills/libnn-optimizer/skill.md` - The full skill definition, especially the Phase 2 section.

Report: for each file, summarize its purpose, key functions, config reading pattern, and any hardcoded paths. Keep it under 600 words total.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile my findings into a comprehensive report.

## Summary Report: libnn-optimizer Baseline Phase Infrastructure

### 1. **validate_config.py** (450 lines)
**Purpose:** Sequential 7-step validation of the optimization environment before any work.

**Key Functionality:**
- **Config Reading:** Uses Python `tomllib.load()` to parse `opt/config.toml` and validates required sections: `[toolchain]` (bin_dir, prefix), `[target]` (cpu, fpga_config), `[qemu]` (binary, vlen, timeout), `[fpga]` (host, port, timeout), `[limits]` (max_iterations, min_improvement_pct).
- **GDB-nopython Symlink:** Lines 118-126 search for `{prefix}gdb-nopython` in toolchain; if missing, warns that system GDB will be used. Later (lines 386-391), if not found, creates a symlink `{prefix}gdb-nopython -> gdb` in a temp directory and prepends tmpdir to PATH so the expect script finds it.
- **Toolchain/QEMU:** Tests gcc exists, runs Andes flags (-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector), verifies QEMU binary and config exist.
- **FPGA Setup:** Parses hardcoded QEMU config at `libnn/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg`. Reads FPGA .inc file to extract hardware features (vlen, dlen, elen, cache sizes, ISA support). Checks FPGA TCP connectivity. Runs hello world on FPGA via expect script wrapper.

**Hardcoded Paths:**
- QEMU config: `libnn/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg` (line 182)
- LD script: `libnn/project/ax45mpv/ae350-xip_hvm.ld` (line 334)
- Sim wrapper: `libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (line 335)
- Expect script: `libnn/project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp` (line 394)
- First_Reset marker: `libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done` (line 375)

---

### 2. **run_qemu_test.sh** (146 lines)
**Purpose:** Compile and run a single libnn unit test on Andes QEMU to verify correctness.

**Flow:**
1. Takes test name (e.g., `conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any`) and optional flags (--strace, --vlen, --timeout).
2. Compiles test source `Examples/unit_func/t_<test>.c` against `libnn.a` with flags: `-O3 -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0`.
3. Runs QEMU from `Examples/unit_func/` directory (so semihosting relative paths work).
4. With --strace: captures fd 9 writes (semihosting on LLM sandbox) via `strace -f -e trace=write` and parses for "accuracy checking pass/fail".
5. Without --strace: direct run, output flows to terminal.

**Hardcoded Paths:**
- Toolchain: `/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin` (line 47)
- QEMU: `/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (line 48)
- QEMU config: `project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg` (line 49)

---

### 3. **build_libnn.sh** (34 lines)
**Purpose:** Build libnn.a for AX45MPV with RVV (VLEN=1024).

**Flow:**
1. Prepends Andes toolchain to PATH: `/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin`.
2. Calls `/usr/bin/bash build.sh AX45MPV` from libnn root.
3. Outputs: `libnn.a` and `libnn.objdump` at repo root.

---

### 4. **test_perf.sh** (323 lines)
**Purpose:** Collect libnn performance metrics on FPGA board. Supports full test suite or single-function mode.

**MYPC/MYPORT Setup (lines 54-59):**
- Default MYPC: `sw-boards.andestech.com` (via env default)
- Default MYPORT: `1115`
- Sourced from env or hardcoded defaults
- Used by sim wrapper (lines 380-382) as target TCP endpoint for FPGA GDB connection

**Single-Function Mode (lines 263-306):**
- Triggered when `$4` (SINGLE_FUNC argument) is non-empty
- Compiles `Examples/unit_func/t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.c` with flags: `-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror -Wl,-gc-sections -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1 -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS`
- INC_DIR (line 281): `-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I$(${COMPILER_NAME} -print-sysroot)/../../riscv64-elf/include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include`
- Runs sim wrapper: `${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_FILE}`
- **Performance Parsing (lines 299-303):** Grep test_perf.log for 'inst' and 'cycle' keywords, extract 5th field via awk, store as integers. Pattern: `grep -w 'inst' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}'`

**Full Test Suite Mode (line 311):**
- Delegates to `test.sh` with flags `-DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS`
- Looks for performance.csv in output folder (lines 319-322)

---

### 5. **resolve_function.py** (69 lines)
**Purpose:** Resolve short user pattern to full libnn perf test function name.

**Config Reading Pattern:** Sets LIBNN_ROOT via path traversal: `OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))` then `LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "../../libnn"))`. No TOML reading—searches filesystem directly.

**Matching:** Glob `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_*.c`, extract filename stems, regex-match user tokens space-separated against function names (case-insensitive). Returns 0 (one match), 1 (zero), or 2 (multiple, prompt user).

---

### 6. **SKILL.md** (209 lines)
**Phase 2: Baseline** (lines 109-131):
1. Rebuild baseline via `build.sh <cpu>` (no agent edits yet)
2. QEMU correctness strace test; accept only "accuracy checking pass"
3. FPGA perf: cd to `build_perf` dir, run `test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>`
4. Parse `performance.csv` for cycles/instructions
5. Write baseline to state.json, set phase to "iterate"

---

### Key Takeaway
The baseline phase validates everything (config, toolchain, QEMU, FPGA), then measures unmodified library on FPGA via a simulation wrapper connected to real board at `MYPC:MYPORT`, parsing cycles/instructions from test output or performance.csv.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Validate config.toml step-by-step. Each step must pass before the next runs."""
3	
4	import os
5	import sys
6	import subprocess
7	import tempfile
8	import tomllib
9	import re
10	import shutil
11	import socket
12	
13	SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
14	OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
15	CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "config.toml")
16	LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "../../libnn"))
17	
18	GREEN = "\033[92m"
19	RED = "\033[91m"
20	YELLOW = "\033[93m"
21	RESET = "\033[0m"
22	BOLD = "\033[1m"
23	
24	def ok(msg):
25	    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")
26	
27	def fail(msg):
28	    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")
29	
30	def warn(msg):
31	    print(f"  {YELLOW}[WARN]{RESET} {msg}")
32	
33	def header(msg):
34	    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")
35	
36	def die(msg):
37	    fail(msg)
38	    sys.exit(1)
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Step 1: Load config.toml, verify all required keys exist
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	def step_load_config():
45	    header("Step 1: Load config.toml")
46	
47	    if not os.path.isfile(CONFIG_PATH):
48	        die(f"config.toml not found at {CONFIG_PATH}")
49	
50	    ok(f"Found {CONFIG_PATH}")
51

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Validate config.toml step-by-step. Each step must pass before the next runs."""

import os
import sys
import subprocess
import tempfile
import tomllib
import re
import shutil
import socket

SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "config.toml")
LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "../../libnn"))

GREEN = "\033[92m"
RED = "\033[91m"
YELLOW = "\033[93m"
RESET = "\033[0m"
BOLD = "\033[1m"

def ok(msg):
    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")

def fail(msg):
    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")

def warn(msg):
    print(f"  {YELLOW}[WARN]{RESET} {msg}")

def header(msg):
    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")

def die(msg):
    fail(msg)
    sys.exit(1)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Step 1: Load config.toml, verify all required keys exist
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def step_load_config():
    header("Step 1: Load config.toml")

    if not os.path.isfile(CONFIG_PATH):
        die(f"config.toml not found at {CONFIG_PATH}")

    ok(f"Found {CONFIG_PATH}")

    with open(CONFIG_PATH, "rb") as f:
        cfg = tomllib.load(f)

    required = {
        "toolchain": ["bin_dir", "prefix"],
        "target": ["cpu", "fpga_config"],
        "qemu": ["binary", "vlen", "timeout"],
        "fpga": ["host", "port", "timeout"],
        "limits": ["max_iterations", "min_improvement_pct"],

155	8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.

160	2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.

202	2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
203	3. **QEMU is for correctness only** -- FPGA cycle counts are ground truth.

---
name: libnn-optimizer
description: |
  Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
  in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
  libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
  optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
  or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
  "libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
  "phase validate/gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
  opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
argument-hint: "[validate|gather|baseline|iterate|status|reset] [pattern]"
allowed-tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Edit, Write, Agent
---

# libnn Optimizer

Autonomous optimization of libnn NN operator functions targeting RISC-V FPGA hardware.
Six-phase FSM: validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate -> evaluate -> done.
State persisted in `opt/state.json`.

## Commands

- `/libnn-optimizer validate` - Run Phase 0: config validation (pass -> gather, fail -> report)
- `/libnn-optimizer gather <pattern>` - Run Phase 1: resolve function via pattern match, parse task spec + target features
- `/libnn-optimizer baseline` - Run Phase 2: measure unmodified library
- `/libnn-optimizer iterate` - Run Phase 3: one optimization iteration
- `/libnn-optimizer status` - Show current state from opt/state.json
- `/libnn-optimizer reset` - Reset state to `validate` phase

Arguments received: `$ARGUMENTS`

## Project Paths

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `$CWD/opt/config.toml` | Target config (user-owned, NEVER modify) |
| `$CWD/opt/state.json` | Optimization state (LLM reads/writes) |
| `$CWD/opt/script/` | Validation and helper scripts |
| `$CWD/opt/fpga_config/` | FPGA Verilog .inc files |
| `$CWD/opt/patches/` | Per-iteration diffs (audit trail) |
| `$CWD/opt/build/` | Build artifacts |
| `$CWD/opt/log/` | Build/test/perf logs |
| `$CWD/plan.md` | Full implementation plan |
| `/home/nick/work/libnn/` | libnn source tree |

## Phase 0: Validate

Initial FSM phase. Validates config.toml and all referenced resources before any optimization work.

**Entry condition:** `phase == "validate"` (initial state) or `/libnn-optimizer validate` command.

```bash
python3 $CWD/opt/script/validate_config.py --strace
```

**On pass ("All steps passed"):**
1. Print full output to the user.
2. Write/update `opt/state.json` setting `phase` to `"gather"`.
3. Report: validation passed, ready for gather phase.

**On fail (any step fails):**
1. Print full output to the user.
2. Keep `phase` as `"validate"` in state.json (or do not advance).
3. Report which steps failed. Do NOT proceed to gather.

## Phase 1: Gather

Parse task spec and target features. No builds.

0. **Resolve function name** from user-supplied pattern:
   ```bash
   python3 $CWD/opt/script/resolve_function.py <pattern>
   ```
   - Exit 0 (one match): use the printed function name, proceed.
   - Exit 2 (multiple matches): show the numbered list to user, ask them to pick. Re-run with a narrower pattern or use the exact name.
   - Exit 1 (no match): report error, do NOT proceed.

   Pattern examples: `conv_dw u8 asym`, `relu_s8`, `sigmoid`.

1. **Read config.toml.** Extract toolchain PATH, QEMU binary, FPGA host:port, CPU, VLEN.

2. **Read FPGA .inc file** (path from `[target].fpga_config`, relative to `opt/`). Extract:
   - Vector: `NDS_VLEN`, `NDS_DLEN`, `NDS_ELEN`
   - D-cache: `NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_DCACHE_WAY`
   - I-cache: `NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE`
   - L2: `NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB`, `NDS_L2C_WAY`, `NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH`
   - ISA: `NDS_RVV_SUPPORT`, `NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT`, `NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT`, `NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT`, `NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT`
   - Bus: `NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH`

   Parse using regex: `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+(\d+) `` for integers, `` `define\s+<KEY>_INT\s+(\d+) `` or `` `define\s+<KEY>\s+"(yes|no)" `` for booleans.

3. **Read source_input** (perf test C file):
   Parse `#define` block from `Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c`. Extract shapes (INPUT_X, INPUT_Y, INPUT_CH, KERNEL_X, etc.), quantization params (OUT_SHIFT, OUT_MULT, offsets, activation min/max), buffer sizes. Resolve function name from `riscv_nn_*()` call site.

4. **Locate baseline source** at `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`. Record path.

5. **Derive optimization macro:** `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC_UPPER>`.

6. **Write state.json.** Set `phase` to `"baseline"`.

**Verification:** state.json must contain:
- All shapes from the perf test `#define` block
- `target_features.vlen == 1024`
- `target_features.dcache_kb == 32`
- `target_features.l2_kb == 8192`
- Correct `source_file` path under `Source/`

## Phase 2: Baseline

Measure unmodified library on target.

1. Rebuild baseline:
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/build.sh <cpu>
   ```

2. QEMU correctness (strace mode for semihosting fd 9):
   ```bash
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/qemu-validate/run_qemu_test.sh <test> --strace --vlen <vlen>
   ```
   Accept only "accuracy checking pass". Failure aborts -- baseline is broken.

3. FPGA perf measurement:
   ```bash
   mkdir -p /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf && cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf
   /usr/bin/bash /home/nick/work/libnn/test_perf.sh ax45mpv riscv64-elf-gcc BOARD <function>
   ```
   Parse `performance.csv` for cycles and instructions.

4. Write `baseline` to state.json. Initialize empty `history`. Set `phase` to `"iterate"`.

## Phase 3: Iterate

One iteration = one candidate optimization. Loop until `max_iterations` or no improvement >= `min_improvement_pct` for 2 consecutive attempts.

Per iteration:

1. **Plan** - Inspect current best source (or baseline on iter 1). Pick one concrete change. Record in `notes`.

2. **Edit** - Modify `Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c`: add optimized path under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`. Replace prior iteration's block if present. Baseline `#else` stays byte-for-byte intact.

3. **Build** - Rebuild `libnn.a` twice:
   - Without macro (sanity -- baseline still compiles)
   - With `-DENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>` for perf

4. **Correctness** - QEMU strace test. Fail -> `accepted=false`, skip perf.

5. **Perf** - `test_perf.sh`. Collect cycles/instructions.

6. **Commit** - Git commit in libnn tree. Snapshot diff to `opt/patches/iter_<N>_<func>.patch`.

7. **Accept/reject** - `accepted = pass && cycles < best.cycles`. Update `best` if accepted.

8. Append to `history`. Persist state.json.

## Phase 4: Evaluate

1. Restore `best.commit` in libnn worktree.
2. Rerun full QEMU suite for touched functions.
3. Rerun FPGA perf, confirm within +/- 0.5% of `best.cycles`.
4. Emit `opt/report.md`: baseline -> best cycles, speedup, commit SHA, rationale.
6. Set `phase` to `"done"`.

## Phase 5: Done

Terminal. Human reviews `opt/report.md`. Winning commits in libnn tree are the deliverable.

## State Machine

Read `opt/state.json` field `phase` to determine where to resume:

```
validate -> gather -> baseline -> iterate (loop) -> evaluate -> done
```

## State File Format

```json
{
  "mode": "single_function",
  "function": "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any",
  "source_input": "Examples/unit_performance/t_pf_<func>.c",
  "source_file": "Source/<Category>/riscv_nn_<func>.c",
  "macro": "ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>",
  "phase": "validate|gather|baseline|iterate|evaluate|done",
  "iteration": 0,
  "baseline": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0},
  "best": {"cycles": 0, "instructions": 0, "iteration": 0, "commit": ""},
  "history": [],
  "shapes": {},
  "quant_params": {},
  "data_types": {"input": "", "weight": "", "output": ""},
  "buffer_sizes": {},
  "target_features": {}
}
```

## Key Rules

1. **Never modify `opt/config.toml`** -- user-owned configuration.
2. **Baseline code is never modified** -- optimized paths go under `#ifdef ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
3. **QEMU is for correctness only** -- FPGA cycle counts are ground truth.
4. **Primary target: AX45MPV VLEN=1024** -- do not sacrifice VLEN=1024 perf.
5. **Use `NDS_VEC_*` macros only** in `ENA_VEC_ISA` blocks. Never `__riscv_*` intrinsics.
6. Macro naming: `ENA_AGENT_OPT_<FUNC>`.
7. Validation is Phase 0 of the FSM. Gather phase requires `phase == "gather"` (validation must have passed).
8. Scripts are the single source of truth for builds and tests -- never construct gcc/QEMU/GDB commands manually.

"""Tests for validate_config.py - reads expected values from config.toml."""

import os
import sys
import subprocess
import tempfile
import tomllib
import unittest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock

OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))

import validate_config as vc

        for section in ["toolchain", "target", "qemu", "fpga", "limits"]:

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Tests for run_baseline.py."""

import json
import os
import sys
import subprocess
import tempfile
import tomllib
import unittest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock, call

TEST_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(TEST_DIR, ".."))
OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "config.toml")
STATE_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "state.json")
LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "../../libnn"))

sys.path.insert(0, SCRIPT_DIR)
import run_baseline as rb


def load_config():
    with open(CONFIG_PATH, "rb") as f:
        return tomllib.load(f)


def load_state():
    with open(STATE_PATH) as f:
        return json.load(f)


CFG = load_config()
STATE = load_state()


class TestLoadInputs(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_load_config(self):
        cfg = rb.load_config(CONFIG_PATH)
        self.assertIn("toolchain", cfg)
        self.assertIn("target", cfg)
        self.assertIn("qemu", cfg)
        self.assertIn("fpga", cfg)

    def test_load_state(self):
        state = rb.load_state(STATE_PATH)
        self.assertIn("function", state)
        self.assertIn("phase", state)

    def test_load_config_missing_file(self):
        with self.assertRaises(SystemExit):
            rb.load_config("/nonexistent/config.toml")

    def test_load_state_missing_file(self):
        with self.assertRaises(SystemExit):
            rb.load_state("/nonexistent/state.json")


class TestSetupToolchain(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_returns_env_with_toolchain_in_path(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        bin_dir = CFG["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
        self.assertTrue(env["PATH"].startswith(bin_dir))

    def test_gdb_nopython_symlink_created(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        prefix = CFG["toolchain"]["prefix"]
        # The symlink dir should be in PATH before toolchain bin_dir
        gdb_nopython = f"{prefix}gdb-nopython"
        # Should be findable via the env PATH
        paths = env["PATH"].split(":")
        found = False
        for p in paths:
            candidate = os.path.join(p, gdb_nopython)
            if os.path.exists(candidate):
                found = True
                break
        self.assertTrue(found, f"{gdb_nopython} not found in PATH")


class TestBuildLibnn(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_build_returns_libnn_path(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        cpu = CFG["target"]["cpu"]
        result = rb.build_libnn(LIBNN_ROOT, cpu, env)
        self.assertTrue(result["success"])
        self.assertTrue(os.path.isfile(result["libnn_path"]))

    def test_build_produces_libnn_a(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        cpu = CFG["target"]["cpu"]
        result = rb.build_libnn(LIBNN_ROOT, cpu, env)
        self.assertTrue(result["libnn_path"].endswith("libnn.a"))


class TestQemuCorrectness(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_qemu_pass(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        func = STATE["function"]
        vlen = CFG["qemu"]["vlen"]
        result = rb.run_qemu_test(LIBNN_ROOT, func, vlen, env)
        self.assertTrue(result["pass"])
        self.assertGreater(result["sub_tests"], 0)

    def test_qemu_bad_function_fails(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        result = rb.run_qemu_test(LIBNN_ROOT, "nonexistent_func", 1024, env)
        self.assertFalse(result["pass"])


class TestFpgaPerf(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_fpga_returns_cycles_and_instructions(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        func = STATE["function"]
        host = CFG["fpga"]["host"]
        port = CFG["fpga"]["port"]
        cpu = CFG["target"]["cpu"]
        result = rb.run_fpga_perf(LIBNN_ROOT, func, cpu, host, port, env)
        self.assertTrue(result["success"])
        self.assertGreater(result["cycles"], 0)
        self.assertGreater(result["instructions"], 0)

    def test_fpga_result_has_accuracy_pass(self):
        env = rb.setup_toolchain_env(CFG)
        func = STATE["function"]
        host = CFG["fpga"]["host"]
        port = CFG["fpga"]["port"]
        cpu = CFG["target"]["cpu"]
        result = rb.run_fpga_perf(LIBNN_ROOT, func, cpu, host, port, env)
        self.assertTrue(result["accuracy_pass"])


class TestParseTestPerfOutput(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_parse_inst_cycle(self):
        log = "The inst count is 474854\nThe cycle count is 626433\naccuracy checking pass\n"
        result = rb.parse_perf_output(log)
        self.assertEqual(result["instructions"], 474854)
        self.assertEqual(result["cycles"], 626433)
        self.assertTrue(result["accuracy_pass"])

    def test_parse_no_match(self):
        log = "some random output\n"
        result = rb.parse_perf_output(log)
        self.assertIsNone(result["instructions"])
        self.assertIsNone(result["cycles"])
        self.assertFalse(result["accuracy_pass"])

    def test_parse_accuracy_fail(self):
        log = "The inst count is 100\nThe cycle count is 200\naccuracy checking fail\n"
        result = rb.parse_perf_output(log)
        self.assertEqual(result["instructions"], 100)
        self.assertEqual(result["cycles"], 200)
        self.assertFalse(result["accuracy_pass"])


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main()

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Phase 2: Baseline measurement for libnn optimizer.

Reads config.toml + state.json, builds unmodified libnn, runs QEMU correctness
check, measures FPGA performance, and outputs JSON result.

Usage:
    python3 run_baseline.py [--skip-qemu] [--skip-fpga] [--json]

Exit codes:
    0 - baseline completed successfully
    1 - build or correctness failure
    2 - FPGA perf measurement failure
    3 - config/state loading error
"""

import json
import os
import re
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
import tomllib

SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "config.toml")
STATE_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "state.json")
LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "../../libnn"))

GREEN = "\033[92m"
RED = "\033[91m"
YELLOW = "\033[93m"
RESET = "\033[0m"
BOLD = "\033[1m"


def ok(msg):
    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")


def fail(msg):
    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")


def header(msg):
    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Loading
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def load_config(path=CONFIG_PATH):
    if not os.path.isfile(path):
        fail(f"config.toml not found: {path}")
        sys.exit(3)
    with open(path, "rb") as f:
        return tomllib.load(f)


def load_state(path=STATE_PATH):
    if not os.path.isfile(path):
        fail(f"state.json not found: {path}")
        sys.exit(3)
    with open(path) as f:
        return json.load(f)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Toolchain environment
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_symlink_tmpdir = None  # module-level to prevent GC cleanup during process


def setup_toolchain_env(cfg):
    """Return an os.environ copy with toolchain bin_dir + gdb-nopython in PATH."""
    global _symlink_tmpdir
    env = os.environ.copy()
    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]

    # Create gdb-nopython symlink if needed
    gdb_real = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb")
    gdb_nopython = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")

    extra_paths = []
    if os.path.isfile(gdb_real) and not os.path.exists(gdb_nopython):
        _symlink_tmpdir = tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="baseline_gdb_")
        link_path = os.path.join(_symlink_tmpdir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
        os.symlink(gdb_real, link_path)
        extra_paths.append(_symlink_tmpdir)
        ok(f"Created gdb-nopython symlink in {_symlink_tmpdir}")

    extra_paths.append(bin_dir)
    env["PATH"] = ":".join(extra_paths) + ":" + env.get("PATH", "")
    return env


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_libnn(libnn_root, cpu, env, extra_flags=None):
    """Build libnn.a for the given CPU. Returns dict with success and libnn_path."""
    header(f"Build libnn for {cpu}")

    cmd = ["/usr/bin/bash", os.path.join(libnn_root, "build.sh"), cpu]
    if extra_flags:
        cmd.append(extra_flags)

    proc = subprocess.run(
        cmd,
        cwd=libnn_root,
        env=env,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        timeout=300,
    )

    libnn_path = os.path.join(libnn_root, "libnn.a")
    if proc.returncode != 0 or not os.path.isfile(libnn_path):
        fail(f"Build failed (exit {proc.returncode})")
        if proc.stderr:
            # Print last 20 lines of stderr for diagnostics
            lines = proc.stderr.strip().split("\n")
            for line in lines[-20:]:
                print(f"    {line}")
        return {"success": False, "libnn_path": "", "log": proc.stderr}

    ok(f"libnn.a built: {libnn_path}")
    return {"success": True, "libnn_path": libnn_path, "log": proc.stdout}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# QEMU correctness
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def run_qemu_test(libnn_root, func, vlen, env, timeout=300):
    """Run QEMU strace correctness test. Returns dict with pass, sub_tests, log."""
    header(f"QEMU correctness: {func} (VLEN={vlen})")

    script = os.path.join(libnn_root, "qemu-validate", "run_qemu_test.sh")
    if not os.path.isfile(script):
        fail(f"run_qemu_test.sh not found: {script}")
        return {"pass": False, "sub_tests": 0, "log": "script not found"}

    cmd = [
        "/usr/bin/bash", script, func,
        "--strace", "--vlen", str(vlen),
        "--timeout", str(timeout),
    ]

    proc = subprocess.run(
        cmd,
        env=env,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        timeout=timeout + 60,
    )

    output = proc.stdout + proc.stderr
    passes = output.count("accuracy checking pass")
    fails = output.count("accuracy checking fail")

    if proc.returncode != 0 or passes == 0 or fails > 0:
        fail(f"QEMU test failed (pass={passes}, fail={fails}, exit={proc.returncode})")
        if "COMPILE_ERROR" in output:
            fail("Compilation error")
        return {"pass": False, "sub_tests": passes, "log": output}

    ok(f"QEMU passed ({passes} sub-tests)")
    return {"pass": True, "sub_tests": passes, "log": output}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FPGA perf
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def parse_perf_output(log):
    """Parse inst/cycle/accuracy from test_perf.sh or FPGA board output."""
    inst_match = re.search(r"inst count is (\d+)", log)
    cycle_match = re.search(r"cycle count is (\d+)", log)
    accuracy_pass = "accuracy checking pass" in log
    accuracy_fail = "accuracy checking fail" in log

    return {
        "instructions": int(inst_match.group(1)) if inst_match else None,
        "cycles": int(cycle_match.group(1)) if cycle_match else None,
        "accuracy_pass": accuracy_pass and not accuracy_fail,
    }


def run_fpga_perf(libnn_root, func, cpu, host, port, env, timeout=600):
    """Run FPGA perf measurement. Returns dict with success, cycles, instructions."""
    header(f"FPGA perf: {func} on {host}:{port}")

    # Ensure libnn_v.a symlink exists (test_perf.sh expects -lnn_v)
    libnn_a = os.path.join(libnn_root, "libnn.a")
    libnn_v = os.path.join(libnn_root, "libnn_v.a")
    if os.path.isfile(libnn_a) and not os.path.isfile(libnn_v):
        os.symlink(libnn_a, libnn_v)

    build_perf = os.path.join(libnn_root, "build_perf")
    os.makedirs(build_perf, exist_ok=True)

    # Clear First_Reset_done
    first_reset = os.path.join(libnn_root, "project", "ax45mpv", "First_Reset_done")
    if os.path.exists(first_reset):
        os.remove(first_reset)

    # Set FPGA env
    perf_env = env.copy()
    perf_env["MYPC"] = host
    perf_env["MYPORT"] = str(port)

    cpu_lower = cpu.lower()
    script = os.path.join(libnn_root, "test_perf.sh")
    cmd = [
        "/usr/bin/bash", script,
        cpu_lower, f"{env.get('TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX', 'riscv64-elf-')}gcc",
        "BOARD", func,
    ]

    # Determine compiler name from config prefix
    prefix = "riscv64-elf-"
    for p in env["PATH"].split(":"):
        candidate = os.path.join(p, f"{prefix}gcc")
        if os.path.isfile(candidate):
            break
    cmd = [
        "/usr/bin/bash", script,
        cpu_lower, f"{prefix}gcc", "BOARD", func,
    ]

    print(f"  cmd: {' '.join(cmd)}")
    print(f"  cwd: {build_perf}")
    print(f"  MYPC={host} MYPORT={port}")

    proc = subprocess.run(
        cmd,
        cwd=build_perf,
        env=perf_env,
        capture_output=True,
        text=True,
        timeout=timeout,
    )

    output = proc.stdout + proc.stderr

    # Parse from combined output
    result = parse_perf_output(output)

    if proc.returncode != 0:
        fail(f"FPGA perf failed (exit {proc.returncode})")
        # Print last 10 lines for diagnostics
        lines = output.strip().split("\n")
        for line in lines[-10:]:
            print(f"    {line}")
        return {
            "success": False,
            "cycles": result["cycles"] or 0,
            "instructions": result["instructions"] or 0,
            "accuracy_pass": result["accuracy_pass"],
            "log": output,
        }

    if result["cycles"] is None or result["instructions"] is None:
        fail("Could not parse cycles/instructions from output")
        return {
            "success": False,
            "cycles": 0,
            "instructions": 0,
            "accuracy_pass": result["accuracy_pass"],
            "log": output,
        }

    if not result["accuracy_pass"]:
        fail("FPGA accuracy check failed")
        return {
            "success": False,
            "cycles": result["cycles"],
            "instructions": result["instructions"],
            "accuracy_pass": False,
            "log": output,
        }

    ok(f"cycles={result['cycles']}  instructions={result['instructions']}")
    return {
        "success": True,
        "cycles": result["cycles"],
        "instructions": result["instructions"],
        "accuracy_pass": True,
        "log": output,
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main():
    import argparse

    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Phase 2: Baseline measurement")
    parser.add_argument("--skip-qemu", action="store_true", help="Skip QEMU correctness")
    parser.add_argument("--skip-fpga", action="store_true", help="Skip FPGA perf")
    parser.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="Output JSON result to stdout")
    parser.add_argument("--extra-cflags", default=None, help="Extra CFLAGS for build")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    print(f"{BOLD}libnn baseline measurement{RESET}")
    print(f"Config: {CONFIG_PATH}")
    print(f"State:  {STATE_PATH}")
    print(f"libnn:  {LIBNN_ROOT}")

    cfg = load_config()
    state = load_state()

    func = state.get("function")
    if not func:
        fail("No function set in state.json")
        sys.exit(3)

    cpu = cfg["target"]["cpu"]
    vlen = cfg["qemu"]["vlen"]
    fpga_host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
    fpga_port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]

    ok(f"Function: {func}")
    ok(f"CPU: {cpu}, VLEN: {vlen}")
    ok(f"FPGA: {fpga_host}:{fpga_port}")

    # Setup
    env = setup_toolchain_env(cfg)

    # Step 1: Build
    build_result = build_libnn(LIBNN_ROOT, cpu, env, args.extra_cflags)
    if not build_result["success"]:
        if args.json:
            print(json.dumps({"status": "build_failed", "error": "build failed"}))
        sys.exit(1)

    # Step 2: QEMU correctness
    qemu_result = {"pass": True, "sub_tests": 0, "log": "skipped"}
    if not args.skip_qemu:
        qemu_result = run_qemu_test(LIBNN_ROOT, func, vlen, env)
        if not qemu_result["pass"]:
            if args.json:
                print(json.dumps({"status": "qemu_failed", "error": "QEMU correctness failed"}))
            sys.exit(1)
    else:
        ok("QEMU skipped (--skip-qemu)")

    # Step 3: FPGA perf
    fpga_result = {"success": True, "cycles": 0, "instructions": 0}
    if not args.skip_fpga:
        fpga_result = run_fpga_perf(LIBNN_ROOT, func, cpu, fpga_host, fpga_port, env)
        if not fpga_result["success"]:
            if args.json:
                print(json.dumps({"status": "fpga_failed", "error": "FPGA perf failed"}))
            sys.exit(2)
    else:
        ok("FPGA skipped (--skip-fpga)")

    # Summary
    header("Baseline complete")
    result = {
        "status": "ok",
        "function": func,
        "cycles": fpga_result["cycles"],
        "instructions": fpga_result["instructions"],
        "qemu_sub_tests": qemu_result["sub_tests"],
    }
    ok(f"Cycles:       {result['cycles']}")
    ok(f"Instructions: {result['instructions']}")
    ok(f"CPI:          {result['cycles'] / max(result['instructions'], 1):.2f}")

    if args.json:
        print(json.dumps(result))

    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== All steps passed ==={RESET}")
    return result


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
